content:
I also wanted to dive deeper into the symbolism of the worn path. While it’s clear that Phoenix’s journey is both literal and metaphorical, I think the path also represents the passage of time and generational sacrifice. Phoenix isn’t only making sacrifices for herself or her grandchild; she’s walking a path that ties her to previous generations. This is reflected in the way she keeps making the journey, despite the toll it takes on her frail body. When she says, “I wasn’t as good as I thought,” it’s a reminder of her vulnerability and age, but also a nod to the fact that the path is not just physical but it carries emotional and spiritual weight, too.


Your analysis of her perseverance and sacrifice is spot-on, and I think these symbols strengthen how Phoenix’s story speaks to a larger narrative of survival and hope.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 8, 2025 6:34PM
Kofi Carr
Karima thank you!
